replies.title: Build and Display a Linked List
replies.short_answer: Use linked-list pointer movement carefully. The key is to keep track of the current node and update next links without losing the remaining list.
explanation: A linked list is not indexed like a Python list. You reach elements by starting at head and following next pointers.
Before changing any next pointer, save the node you still need to visit later.
Empty list and single-node list cases should be checked separately when required.
replies.dry_run: Trace the list on paper using boxes for nodes and arrows for next pointers. Mark head, current, previous, slow, fast, or tail according to the problem.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to return the updated head.
Changing current.next before saving the next node.
Not handling head deletion or empty list cases.
Using Python indexing instead of pointer traversal.
